on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  changes:
    name: Detect changes
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
    outputs:
      swift: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.swift }}
      rust: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.rust }}
      sample-app: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.sample-app }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@de90cc6fb38fc0963ad72b210f1f284cd68cea36 # v3
        id: filter
        with:
          filters: |
            swift:
              - 'macOS/**'
              - 'Package.swift'
              - 'Package.resolved'
            rust:
              - 'windows/**'
              - 'Examples/windows/SampleApp/src-tauri/**'
            sample-app:
              - 'Examples/windows/SampleApp/**'
              - '!Examples/windows/SampleApp/src-tauri/**'

  swift-lint:
    name: Swift Lint
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.swift == 'true'
    runs-on: macos-15
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6

      - name: Install SwiftLint
        run: |
          SWIFTLINT_VERSION=0.57.1
          curl -sL "https://github.com/realm/SwiftLint/releases/download/${SWIFTLINT_VERSION}/portable_swiftlint.zip" -o swiftlint.zip
          unzip -q swiftlint.zip -d swiftlint
          echo "$PWD/swiftlint" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Install SwiftFormat
        run: |
          SWIFTFORMAT_VERSION=0.55.5
          curl -sL "https://github.com/nicklockwood/SwiftFormat/releases/download/${SWIFTFORMAT_VERSION}/swiftformat.zip" -o swiftformat.zip
          unzip -q swiftformat.zip
          chmod +x swiftformat
          echo "$PWD" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: SwiftLint
        run: swiftlint lint --strict --config macOS/.swiftlint.yml macOS/Sources/ macOS/Tests/

      - name: SwiftFormat
        run: swiftformat --lint --config macOS/.swiftformat macOS/Sources/ macOS/Tests/

  swift-test:
    name: Swift Build & Test
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.swift == 'true'
    runs-on: macos-15
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6

      - name: Cache SPM dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@cdf6c1fa76f9f475f3d7449005a359c84ca0f306 # v5
        with:
          path: |
            .build
            ~/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm
          key: spm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('Package.resolved') }}
          restore-keys: spm-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Build
        run: swift build

      - name: Test
        run: swift test

  rust-lint:
    name: Rust Lint (Linux)
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.rust == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6

      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: clippy, rustfmt

      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@779680da715d629ac1d338a641029a2f4372abb5 # v2
        with:
          workspaces: "windows -> windows/target"

      - name: Check formatting
        working-directory: windows
        run: cargo fmt --all --check

      - name: Clippy (core — platform-agnostic)
        run: >
          cargo clippy --manifest-path windows/Cargo.toml
          -p audio-capture-core
          -- -D warnings -W clippy::undocumented_unsafe_blocks

  rust-clippy-windows:
    name: Rust Clippy (Windows)
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.rust == 'true'
    runs-on: windows-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6

      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: clippy

      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@779680da715d629ac1d338a641029a2f4372abb5 # v2
        with:
          workspaces: "windows -> windows/target"

      - name: Clippy (windows crate)
        run: >
          cargo clippy --manifest-path windows/Cargo.toml
          -p audio-capture-windows
          -- -D warnings

  rust-test:
    name: Rust Test (${{ matrix.package }})
    needs: changes
    # No job-level if: matrix jobs must always run so each expanded name
    # (e.g. "Rust Test (audio-capture-core)") is individually reported to
    # GitHub. A job-level if causes GitHub to report only the unexpanded
    # template name, which never satisfies the required status checks.
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - package: audio-capture-core
            os: ubuntu-latest
          - package: audio-capture-windows
            os: windows-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6

      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        if: needs.changes.outputs.rust == 'true'

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  changes:
    name: Detect changes
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
    outputs:
      mac: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.mac }}
      core: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.core }}
      has-cargo: ${{ steps.cargo-check.outputs.exists }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v4
      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@de90cc6fb38fc0963ad72b210f1f284cd68cea36 # v3
        id: filter
        with:
          filters: |
            mac:
              - 'mac/**'
            core:
              - 'core/**'
      - name: Check for core/Cargo.toml
        id: cargo-check
        run: |
          if [ -f core/Cargo.toml ]; then
            echo "exists=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "exists=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

  swift-lint:
    name: Swift Lint
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.mac == 'true'
    runs-on: macos-26
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v4

      - name: Install SwiftLint
        run: |
          SWIFTLINT_VERSION=0.63.2
          curl -sL "https://github.com/realm/SwiftLint/releases/download/${SWIFTLINT_VERSION}/portable_swiftlint.zip" -o swiftlint.zip
          unzip -q swiftlint.zip -d swiftlint
          echo "$PWD/swiftlint" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Install SwiftFormat
        run: |
          SWIFTFORMAT_VERSION=0.59.1
          curl -sL "https://github.com/nicklockwood/SwiftFormat/releases/download/${SWIFTFORMAT_VERSION}/swiftformat.zip" -o swiftformat.zip
          unzip -q swiftformat.zip
          chmod +x swiftformat
          echo "$PWD" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: SwiftLint
        run: swiftlint lint --strict --config mac/.swiftlint.yml mac/PabloCompanion/

      - name: SwiftFormat
        run: swiftformat --lint --config mac/.swiftformat mac/PabloCompanion/

  swift-build:
    name: Swift Build
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.mac == 'true' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
    runs-on: macos-26
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v4

      - name: Cache SPM dependencies
        id: spm-cache
        uses: actions/cache@6849a6489940f00c2f30c0fb92c6274307ccb58a # v4
        with:
          path: mac/SourcePackages
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-spm-${{ hashFiles('mac/PabloCompanion.xcodeproj/project.xcworkspace/xcshareddata/swiftpm/Package.resolved') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-spm-

      - name: Build
        run: |
          DISABLE_PKGS=${{ steps.spm-cache.outputs.cache-hit == 'true' && '-disableAutomaticPackageResolution' || '' }}
          xcodebuild -project mac/PabloCompanion.xcodeproj \
            -scheme PabloCompanion \
            -destination 'platform=macOS' \
            -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath mac/SourcePackages \
            $DISABLE_PKGS \
            CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="" \
            CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO \
            CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO \
            build | xcpretty

  rust-lint:
    name: Rust Lint
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.core == 'true' && needs.changes.outputs.has-cargo == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v4

      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: clippy, rustfmt

      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@779680da715d629ac1d338a641029a2f4372abb5 # v2

      - name: Check formatting
        working-directory: core
        run: cargo fmt --all --check

      - name: Clippy
        working-directory: core
        run: cargo clippy -- -D warnings -W clippy::undocumented_unsafe_blocks

  rust-test:
    name: Rust Test
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.core == 'true' && needs.changes.outputs.has-cargo == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v4

      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@779680da715d629ac1d338a641029a2f4372abb5 # v2

      - name: Test
        working-directory: core
        run: cargo test

  dependency-review:
    name: Dependency Review
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:

      - uses: actions/dependency-review-action@05fe4576374b728f0c523d6a13d64c25081e0803 # v4
        with:

          deny-licenses: GPL-2.0, GPL-3.0, AGPL-3.0

name: CodeQL

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 8 * * 3" # Wednesday 8AM UTC
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  security-events: write

jobs:
  changes:
    name: Detect Swift changes
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
    outputs:
      # Always run on push/schedule; on PRs only run if Swift files changed
      swift: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' || steps.filter.outputs.swift == 'true' }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@de90cc6fb38fc0963ad72b210f1f284cd68cea36 # v3.0.2
        id: filter
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        with:
          filters: |
            swift:
              - 'mac/**'

  analyze-swift:
    name: CodeQL (swift)
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.swift == 'true'
    runs-on: macos-26
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - name: Initialize CodeQL
        uses: github/codeql-action/init@89a39a4e59826350b863aa6b6252a07ad50cf83e # v4.32.4
        with:
          languages: swift
          queries: security-extended

      - name: Cache SPM dependencies
        id: spm-cache
        uses: actions/cache@cdf6c1fa76f9f475f3d7449005a359c84ca0f306 # v5.0.3
        with:
          path: mac/SourcePackages
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-spm-${{ hashFiles('mac/PabloCompanion.xcodeproj/project.xcworkspace/xcshareddata/swiftpm/Package.resolved') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-spm-

      - name: Build
        run: |
          DISABLE_PKGS=${{ steps.spm-cache.outputs.cache-hit == 'true' && '-disableAutomaticPackageResolution' || '' }}
          xcodebuild -project mac/PabloCompanion.xcodeproj \
            -scheme PabloCompanion \
            -destination 'platform=macOS' \
            -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath mac/SourcePackages \
            $DISABLE_PKGS \
            CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="" \
            CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO \
            CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO \
            build

      - name: Perform CodeQL Analysis
        uses: github/codeql-action/analyze@89a39a4e59826350b863aa6b6252a07ad50cf83e # v4.32.4
        with:
          category: /language:swift

content.thinking:
The user is asking if it's normal for CodeQL to appear hung on "resolve package graph". This is a known issue with CodeQL and Swift projects, especially with SPM dependencies.

The "resolve package graph" step is part of the CodeQL initialization/autobuild process for Swift. It happens during the CodeQL analysis phase when CodeQL is trying to build the project and resolve all Swift Package Manager dependencies.

This can appear hung for several reasons:
1. **SPM network requests**: If `disableAutomaticPackageResolution` is not set (i.e., cache miss), SPM may be making network requests to resolve packages, which can be slow


2. **CodeQL Swift analysis is notoriously slow**: The resolve package graph step is a known bottleneck that can take anywhere from 5-15 minutes or longer, especially on macOS runners

, and it's not actually hung despite appearing that way.
3. **CodeQL runs its own build internally**: Even after xcodebuild completes with caching, CodeQL's analyze step performs its own package resolution and build as part of its analysis process, which is why the workflow seems to stall at that point.

content.text:
Yes, completely normal — it's a well-known CodeQL Swift quirk.

"Resolve package graph" is CodeQL's internal step during the `analyze` action where it re-resolves SPM dependencies for its own build database, separate from the xcodebuild step you ran earlier. For Swift projects it routinely sits there for 5–15+ minutes and looks completely frozen.

A few things that can make it worse:
- **Branch-pinned dependencies** — network check required on every run. Your memory notes `AudioCaptureKit` was updated to `pablo-health/AudioCaptureKit@v1.0.0` (semver tag) which helps, but CodeQL does its own resolution pass
- **macos-26 runners** — still relatively new, occasional slowness
- **`security-extended` queries** — you're running the extended query suite which adds to total time

It should eventually complete. If it genuinely times out (GitHub default is 6 hours for macOS), that's a different problem — but just appearing stuck for 10–20 minutes is expected for Swift CodeQL.

Yeah, 16 minutes is completely normal for CodeQL Swift. It's one of the slowest language analyzers — the package graph resolution + building a CodeQL database from Swift source + running the extended query suite all add up.

For a project this size you'd typically expect 15–25 minutes total. Nothing to worry about.

content.thinking:
OK, so the `.beads/.gitignore` intentionally ignores the `dolt/` database and other runtime/sync files. It notes that `issues.jsonl` and `interactions.jsonl` are NOT ignored (they're tracked by git by default).

The reason there's no `issues.jsonl` is because beads is in cloud/Dolt mode, so the issues live on the beads server, not in a local JSONL file. The `issues.jsonl` would only exist if they were in `no-db: true` mode.

The user is asking why the issues aren't in source control. The answer is:


In cloud mode, the beads server is the source of truth for issues, whereas switching to `no-db: true` would generate an `issues.jsonl` file that gets committed to git instead. So the current setup deliberately keeps issues on the server rather than in version control.

content.text:
Because you're in **cloud mode** — the beads server is the source of truth, not the filesystem. There's nothing to commit.

If you were in `no-db: true` mode, beads would write an `issues.jsonl` locally, and the `.beads/.gitignore` is explicitly written to *not* ignore it (see line 50-54 — they specifically avoided adding negation patterns so JSONL files get tracked by git).

So you have two options for source-controlled issues:
1. **Switch to no-db mode**: set `no-db: true` in `.beads/config.yaml`, run `bd export` to dump from the server to `issues.jsonl`, then commit it — git becomes the source of truth
2. **Stay in cloud mode**: the beads server is the source of truth, issues aren't in git — which is what you have now

The cloud mode is fine for a team (everyone queries the server), but if you want the issue history in the git repo itself, no-db is the way to go.

file.content:
# Beads - AI-Native Issue Tracking

Welcome to Beads! This repository uses **Beads** for issue tracking - a modern, AI-native tool designed to live directly in your codebase alongside your code.

## What is Beads?

Beads is issue tracking that lives in your repo, making it perfect for AI coding agents and developers who want their issues close to their code. No web UI required - everything works through the CLI and integrates seamlessly with git.

**Learn more:** [github.com/steveyegge/beads](https://github.com/steveyegge/beads)

## Quick Start

### Essential Commands

```bash
# Create new issues
bd create "Add user authentication"

# View all issues
bd list

# View issue details
bd show <issue-id>

# Update issue status
bd update <issue-id> --status in_progress
bd update <issue-id> --status done

# Sync with Dolt remote
bd dolt push
```

### Working with Issues

Issues in Beads are:
- **Git-native**: Stored in `.beads/issues.jsonl` and synced like code
- **AI-friendly**: CLI-first design works perfectly with AI coding agents
- **Branch-aware**: Issues can follow your branch workflow
- **Always in sync**: Auto-syncs with your commits

## Why Beads?

✨ **AI-Native Design**
- Built specifically for AI-assisted development workflows
- CLI-first interface works seamlessly with AI coding agents
- No context switching to web UIs

🚀 **Developer Focused**
- Issues live in your repo, right next to your code
- Works offline, syncs when you push
- Fast, lightweight, and stays out of your way

🔧 **Git Integration**
- Automatic sync with git commits
- Branch-aware issue tracking
- Intelligent JSONL merge resolution

## Get Started with Beads

Try Beads in your own projects:

```bash
# Install Beads
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/steveyegge/beads/main/scripts/install.sh | bash

# Initialize in your repo
bd init

# Create your first issue
bd create "Try out Beads"
```

## Learn More

- **Documentation**: [github.com/steveyegge/beads/docs](https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/tree/main/docs)
- **Quick Start Guide**: Run `bd quickstart`
- **Examples**: [github.com/steveyegge/beads/examples](https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/tree/main/examples)

---

*Beads: Issue tracking that moves at the speed of thought* ⚡

OK I need to step back and explain what I found before making more changes.

Beads is connected to a **local MySQL-compatible server** at `127.0.0.1:3306` — that's Dolt running as a database server, not a file. The `.beads/dolt` directory is just a config placeholder; the actual data lives in that server.

Setting `no-db: true` doesn't help here because there's no `issues.jsonl` to read from, and beads has no built-in "dump to JSONL" export command. I'd have to hand-craft the JSONL from `bd list --json` output, which risks format mismatches.

For **issues in git source control**, you actually have two real options:

1. **`bd dolt push`** — Dolt is a git-like database, so pushing commits the issue history to a remote Dolt repo. Issues are versioned but in a separate Dolt remote, not your main git repo.

2. **Keep no-db mode but bootstrap the JSONL** — I construct `issues.jsonl` from `bd list --json`, accept some risk of missing internal fields, and commit it. Writes would then go back to the file on every `bd` command.

content.text:
They'd connect to the same Dolt server you're running at `127.0.0.1:3306`. The workflow would be:

1. They clone the repo
2. They run `bd dolt pull` to sync issues from the remote Dolt database
3. When they make changes, `bd dolt push` sends them back

But that requires a **shared remote Dolt database** to be set up — right now your Dolt server is purely local. `bd dolt show` showed no remote configured, just a local connection.

So currently a collaborator can't get the issues at all. You'd need to either:

- **Set up a Dolt remote** (Dolt Hub, self-hosted) and `bd dolt push` to it — then collaborators do `bd dolt pull`
- **Switch to no-db mode** and commit `issues.jsonl` to git — collaborators just `git pull` like normal code

The no-db/JSONL approach is simpler for a small team since it piggybacks on your existing git workflow with no extra infrastructure.

        cmd += ['--description', issue['description']]
    if issue.get('parent'):
        cmd += ['--parent', issue['parent']]

    if r.returncode == 0:
        data = json.loads(r.stdout)
        if isinstance(data, list):
            issues.extend(data)
        else:
            issues.append(data)
    else:
        print(f"WARN: failed to fetch {issue_id}", file=sys.stderr)

print(f"Fetched details for {len(issues)} issues", file=sys.stderr)

# Separate open/closed
open_issues   = [i for i in issues if i['status'] != 'closed']
closed_issues = [i for i in issues if i['status'] == 'closed']
print(f"Open: {len(open_issues)}, Closed: {len(closed_issues)}", file=sys.stderr)

def sort_key(i):
    return (0 if i['issue_type'] == 'epic' else 1, i['priority'])

sorted_issues = sorted(open_issues, key=sort_key) + sorted(closed_issues, key=sort_key)

cmds_create = []
cmds_deps   = []

for issue in sorted_issues:
    cmd = ['bd','create','--silent',
           '--id', issue['id'],
           '--title', issue['title'],
           '--type', issue['issue_type'],
           '--priority', str(issue['priority']),
    ]
    if issue.get('description'):
        cmd += ['--description', issue['description']]
    if issue.get('parent'):
        cmd += ['--parent', issue['parent']]
    cmds_create.append(cmd)

    # blocks deps (parent-child handled via --parent above)
    for dep in issue.get('dependencies', []):
        dep_type = dep.get('type') or dep.get('dependency_type')
        if dep_type == 'blocks':
            dep_id = dep.get('depends_on_id') or dep.get('id')
            cmds_deps.append(['bd','dep','add', issue['id'], dep_id, '--type','blocks'])

def shell_quote(s):
    return "'" + s.replace("'", "'\"'\"'") + "'"

with open('/tmp/beads_cmds.sh', 'w') as f:
    f.write('#!/bin/bash\nset -e\n\n')
    f.write('echo "Phase 1: Creating issues..."\n')
    for cmd in cmds_create:
        parts = [shell_quote(a) if any(c in a for c in " '\"()\n/\\[]") else a for a in cmd]
        f.write(' '.join(parts) + '\n')
    f.write('\necho "Phase 2: Adding deps..."\n')
    for cmd in cmds_deps:
        f.write(' '.join(cmd) + '\n')
    f.write('\necho "Done!"\n')

print(f"Script: {len(cmds_create)} creates, {len(cmds_deps)} dep adds", file=sys.stderr)

cat > /tmp/migrate_beads2.py << 'PYEOF'
import json, subprocess, sys

result = subprocess.run(
    ['bd','sql','SELECT id FROM issues ORDER BY issue_type DESC, priority'],
    capture_output=True, text=True
)
all_ids = [l.strip() for l in result.stdout.strip().split('\n') if l.strip().startswith('PABLO-D-')]

issues = []
for issue_id in all_ids:
    r = subprocess.run(['bd','show',issue_id,'--json'], capture_output=True, text=True)
    if r.returncode == 0:
        data = json.loads(r.stdout)
        issues.extend(data if isinstance(data, list) else [data])

open_issues   = [i for i in issues if i['status'] != 'closed']
closed_issues = [i for i in issues if i['status'] == 'closed']

def sort_key(i):
    return (0 if i['issue_type'] == 'epic' else 1, i['priority'])

sorted_issues = sorted(open_issues, key=sort_key) + sorted(closed_issues, key=sort_key)

def shell_quote(s):
    return "'" + s.replace("'", "'\"'\"'") + "'"

cmds_create = []
cmds_deps   = []

for issue in sorted_issues:
    cmd = ['bd','create','--silent',
           '--id', issue['id'],
           '--title', issue['title'],
           '--type', issue['issue_type'],
           '--priority', str(issue['priority']),
    ]
    if issue.get('description'):
        cmd += ['--description', issue['description']]
    # No --parent here; handled as dep below
    cmds_create.append(cmd)

    for dep in issue.get('dependencies', []):
        dep_type = dep.get('type') or dep.get('dependency_type')
        dep_id = dep.get('depends_on_id') or dep.get('id')
        if dep_type in ('blocks', 'parent-child'):
            cmds_deps.append(['bd','dep','add', issue['id'], dep_id, '--type', dep_type])

with open('/tmp/beads_cmds2.sh', 'w') as f:
    f.write('#!/bin/bash\nset -e\n\n')
    f.write('echo "Phase 1: Creating issues..."\n')
    for cmd in cmds_create:
        parts = [shell_quote(a) if any(c in a for c in " '\"()\n/\\[]") else a for a in cmd]
        f.write(' '.join(parts) + '\n')
    f.write('\necho "Phase 2: Adding deps..."\n')
    for cmd in cmds_deps:
        f.write(' '.join(cmd) + '\n')
    f.write('\necho "Done!"\n')

print(f"{len(cmds_create)} creates, {len(cmds_deps)} dep adds", file=sys.stderr)
PYEOF
python3 /tmp/migrate_beads2.py
